historyState.remoteSessionMissing: The backend session data was not found. If you just reconnected an SSH remote workspace, close and reopen the remote project, or create a new session and try again.

export.redactPathsHelp: Replace workspace and file paths when copying Markdown.

coverage.partialNotice: Some metrics were not reported by this session or provider. Hover underlined values for the specific reason.

loading.title: Generating usage report
loading.description: Reading local session records and preparing a privacy-safe summary.

empty.modelsDescription: Model rows appear after calls report token usage.
empty.toolsDescription: Tool rows appear after the session runs tools.
empty.filesDescription: No file-edit records were found for this session.
empty.errorsDescription: No sampled tool or model errors were recorded.
empty.slowestDescription: No timed turn, model-call, or tool spans were recorded.

help.wall: Span from the first recorded turn start to the last recorded turn end. Idle gaps can be included.
help.active: Union of recorded turn spans that produced reportable activity. It can include orchestration or waiting inside a turn.
help.timeShare: Share of recorded turn time. Model and tool spans may overlap, so this is only an approximate indicator.
help.modelRoundTime: Recorded model-round duration from persisted runtime metadata or start/end timestamps, not pure provider streaming or throughput time. The percentage uses recorded turn time and is approximate.
help.toolTime: Recorded tool-call duration. The percentage uses recorded turn time and is approximate.
help.cachedTokens: The provider did not report cache-read token metadata for this session. Total token counts are still shown when available.
help.cachedTokensPartial: Only some calls reported cache-read token metadata, so the cached-token total covers those calls only.
help.toolDuration: Total recorded time for this tool name in the report scope. It may include queueing, preflight checks, confirmation wait, and execution.
help.toolP95: 95th percentile of recorded total call durations for this tool. It is shown only after at least two timed calls.
help.toolExecution: Time spent inside the tool execution phase after queueing, preflight checks, and confirmation wait. Older sessions may not have this field.
help.legacyModel: Older sessions did not store per-round model names, so this report cannot identify the exact model.
help.inferredModel: Inferred from the session model setting because older turns did not store per-round model names.
help.filesUnavailable: No file snapshot or file-edit tool record was found for this session.
help.filesNoRecordedChanges: BitFun did not detect file changes in this session. This is expected when the agent did not edit files.
help.filesRemoteUnavailable: No remote snapshot summary was found for this session. File rows can still appear from recognized file-edit tool records.
help.filesNotTracked: No local snapshot or identifiable file-edit tool record was found for this session.
help.fileTurnIndexes: Turn indexes are not recorded when file activity comes from a summary instead of per-turn operation records.
help.fileOperationIds: Operation IDs are not recorded when the report cannot link this row back to individual tool operations.
help.fileDiffUnavailable: Diff links require a snapshot-backed file row and a visible file path.
help.errors: Counts model turns that ended in error plus tool calls whose result was unsuccessful. It does not include raw provider responses, tool inputs, or command output.
help.toolErrors: Tool errors count tool calls whose persisted result is marked unsuccessful.
help.modelErrors: Model errors count dialog turns that ended in the error state.
help.errorExamples: Examples are grouped by safe labels only; raw provider responses, tool inputs, and command output stay out of the report.
help.errorExampleRow: Safe grouped label for a model/runtime error or failing tool type.
help.errorExampleCount: Number of matching errors in this report scope.
help.slowestSpans: Slow spans are derived from recorded turn, model-call, and tool timestamps. They help identify time sinks, not exact provider latency.
help.slowestModelCall: Model call in this turn. Model: {{model}}.
help.slowestToolCall: Tool call details identify whether time was spent waiting, confirming, or executing.

runtime.tooltip: Generate a usage report in this chat

privacy.title: Privacy-safe report
privacy.summary: Prompts, tool inputs, command outputs, and file contents are not included.

threadGoal.menuEmpty: No thread goal yet. Set an objective to keep the agent focused across turns.

steps.continue: After each turn, auto-continue toward the objective
steps.audit: Verify every requirement against current evidence (not surface-level done)
steps.complete: When proven, the agent calls update_goal to mark Complete

note.active: While active, each finished turn auto-continues toward the goal (up to 100 times) until the agent calls update_goal(complete) or the limit is reached.
note.complete: Marked complete. Edit or clear the goal to start different work.
note.paused: Paused — continuation and completion checks are stopped.
note.blocked: Blocked — resume with /goal resume after you unblock or the environment changes.
note.usageLimited: Paused due to usage limits — /goal resume after quota recovers.
note.budgetLimited: Token budget reached — edit the goal or clear and set a new one.

threadGoal.editHint: Type an objective and save. The agent will continue toward it when idle.
threadGoal.resumeHint: Mark it active and continue when the session is idle. You can leave it paused and use /goal resume later.
threadGoal.stripTooltipEmpty: Set or view the thread goal

message.rollbackTo: Rollback to before turn {{index}} (delete this and following turns)

message.rollbackPanelBulletRestore: Restore files to the state before turn {{index}}
message.rollbackPanelBulletUndo: Undo all file changes from that turn onward
message.rollbackPanelBulletHistory: Keep the session history (you can roll forward again anytime)
message.rollbackSuccess: Rolled back and deleted subsequent dialog records
message.editDisabledImages: Image messages cannot be edited yet
message.editDisabledRemote: Remote sessions do not support message editing yet
message.editDisabledLocalCommand: Local command messages cannot be edited
message.editDisabledHistoryNotReady: Session history is not ready yet
message.editDialogBulletFiles: Restore files to the state before this turn
message.editDialogBulletHistory: Delete this turn and all following dialog records
message.editDialogBulletRerun: Start a new turn with the edited message

modeDescriptions.agentic: Full-featured AI assistant with access to all tools for comprehensive software development tasks
modeDescriptions.Multitask: Multitask mode: decompose work into orthogonal branches and proactively use subagents in parallel when it helps
modeDescriptions.Claw: Personal assistant mode for dedicated assistant workspaces and everyday task support
modeDescriptions.Plan: Plan first, execute later — clarify requirements and create an implementation plan before coding
modeDescriptions.debug: Evidence-driven systematic debugging: form hypotheses, gather runtime evidence, and fix with confidence
modeDescriptions.Cowork: Collaborative mode: clarify first, track progress lightly, verify outcomes anytime
modeDescriptions.ComputerUse: Dedicated desktop automation agent for perceiving and operating apps, browsers, and OS UI
modeDescriptions.DeepResearch: Deep research with parallel sub-agents: dispatches multiple agents to investigate concurrently, producing comprehensive sourced reports
modeDescriptions.Team: Virtual engineering team: CEO, Eng Manager, Designer, QA Lead, Security Officer, Release Engineer — orchestrated through a full sprint workflow

manualCancel.title: Review stopped
manualCancel.description: You stopped this Deep Review. Completed reviewer output is preserved, and you can continue the remaining work when ready.

resultRecovery.title: Deep review needs a report
resultRecovery.missingSubmitCodeReview: The review finished, but BitFun did not receive a structured result to confirm. Continue preserves completed reviewer work and asks the agent to submit the missing report.
resultRecovery.invalidSubmitCodeReview: The review returned a result BitFun could not read. Continue preserves completed reviewer work and asks the agent to repair the report.
resultRecovery.wrongReviewMode: The review returned a standard Code Review result. Continue preserves completed reviewer work and asks the agent to submit the Deep Review report.

capacityQueue.title: Reviewers waiting for capacity
capacityQueue.detail: Queue wait does not count against reviewer runtime.
capacityQueue.activeReviewerDetail: Queued reviewers start when a running reviewer frees capacity. Queue wait does not count against reviewer runtime.
capacityQueue.longLaunchBatchWaitDetail: This reviewer has waited longer than the configured queue window because an earlier reviewer batch is still running. You can keep waiting, pause the queue, cancel queued reviewers, or open Review settings.
capacityQueue.providerDetail: BitFun is waiting for temporary model capacity. This wait does not count against reviewer runtime.

reasonDetails.providerRateLimit: The model provider is rate-limiting requests. BitFun will wait and continue when capacity returns.
reasonDetails.providerConcurrencyLimit: The model provider rejected another concurrent reviewer. BitFun will retry after capacity opens.
reasonDetails.retryAfter: The model provider asked BitFun to retry later. Waiting here avoids spending reviewer runtime while the provider cools down.
reasonDetails.localConcurrencyCap: The configured Review Team reviewer limit is full. This reviewer will start after an active reviewer finishes.
reasonDetails.launchBatchBlocked: An earlier launch batch is still running. Waiting preserves the planned review order and prevents a later batch from overtaking it.
reasonDetails.temporaryOverload: The model provider reported temporary overload. BitFun will wait, then continue or mark the reviewer as capacity skipped.

capacityQueue.sessionBusy: Your active session is busy. Pause Deep Review or continue later.
capacityQueue.stopHint: Use Stop to interrupt this review queue.
capacityQueue.controlFailed: Queue control is unavailable because this reviewer is missing session, turn, or tool identifiers. Use Stop to interrupt the review, or wait for the queue state to refresh.
capacityQueue.controlFailedWithReason: Queue control failed: {{reason}}. Try again, or use Stop to interrupt the review if the queue is stuck.
capacityQueue.controlPartiallyFailedWithReason: Queue control partly applied; {{failed}} of {{total}} reviewers failed: {{reason}}. Wait for the queue state to refresh, then retry or use Stop if it is stuck.

degradation.reduceReviewersDesc: Skip extra reviewers and keep only core reviewers
degradation.reduceReviewersPending: Reduced reviewer mode will be supported in a future update.
degradation.compressContextDesc: Compress completed reviewer results to free up context space
degradation.compressContextPending: Context compression will be supported in a future update.

launchError.modelConfig: Failed to create review session. Please check model configuration.
launchError.network: Network connection interrupted. Review failed to start.

deepReviewConsent.title: Start Deep Review?
deepReviewConsent.body: Deep Review launches multiple reviewers, so expect more tokens and longer runtime.
deepReviewConsent.sessionConcurrencyBody: The target session already has {{count}} running subagent tasks. Choose a lighter strategy, cancel for now, or continue manually when capacity is free.
deepReviewConsent.strategyOverrideBody: Quick is narrower; Deep covers more.

strategySummaries.quick: Quick keeps built-in target-matched reviewers, skips user-added specialists, and reports reduced coverage.
strategySummaries.normal: Normal stays practical for slower models, limits optional expansion, and uses summary-first on large changes.
strategySummaries.deep: Thorough multi-pass review for broad or risky changes; allows longer runtime and split coverage.

global.title: Other folder

global.description: Use a different folder for Cowork tasks. Outputs will be saved in that folder.

desktop_cleanup.title: Organize desktop screenshots
desktop_cleanup.description: Rename, group, and file recent screenshots.
desktop_cleanup.prompt:
Help me organize recent screenshots on my Desktop.

First, scan my Desktop and count how many screenshots/images are there. Show me:

- Total count

- Date range (oldest to newest)

Then, focus only on screenshots from the last 14 days. For each one:

- Identify what it shows

- Suggest a descriptive filename

- Propose which folder it belongs in (or if it can be deleted)

Group similar screenshots together. Show me the plan before making any changes.

After I approve, start by organizing just 10 files as a preview. If there are more than 10 files, check in with me before continuing with the rest.

vacation_plan.title: Plan my vacation
vacation_plan.description: Build a day-by-day itinerary with budget + backups.
vacation_plan.prompt:
Plan a vacation for me.

Basics:
- From:
- Destinations (optional backups):
- Dates / total days:
- Travelers + preferences:
- Budget range:
- Interests (food / nature / museums / shopping / relax):
- Hard constraints (no early mornings, no long drives, etc.):

Output:
1) 2–3 destination options with pros/cons
2) Best option: day-by-day plan (morning/afternoon/evening)
3) Transport + lodging suggestions
4) Budget breakdown
5) Rainy-day/contingency alternatives

make_ppt.title: Draft a PPT
make_ppt.description: Outline slides, talking points, and visuals.
make_ppt.prompt:
Help me create a PPT deck.

Topic:
Audience:
Time limit:
Use case (status update / pitch / training / sharing):
Preferred style (clean / business / playful / minimal):

Deliverable:
1) Table of contents
2) Slide-by-slide: title + 3–5 bullets
3) Short speaker notes for key slides
4) Visual/chart suggestions (what chart for what message)
5) Export as a PPTX file (save it under artifacts/)

todo_breakdown.title: Break it into todos
todo_breakdown.description: Turn a goal into executable tasks with estimates.
todo_breakdown.prompt:
Break the following goal into an executable todo list with priorities and time estimates.

Goal:
Deadline:
Current status:
Resources (people/budget/tools):
Risks/unknowns:

Output:
1) Milestones
2) Task list (owner / ETA / dependencies / acceptance criteria)
3) Critical path + risks
4) Three small things I can start tomorrow

optimize_week.title: Optimize my week
optimize_week.description: Find patterns across messy notes and transcripts.
optimize_week.prompt:
Help me find patterns and insights across [my voice memos / meeting transcripts / documents / journal entries / specify folder]. I have messy, unstructured files and want to understand what themes are emerging.

First, scan the folder and show me a summary:

- Total files

- Date range (oldest to newest)

- Types of content

Before analyzing, ask me:

- What I'm hoping to discover (recurring themes, contradictions, evolution of thinking, action items, or something else)

- Whether certain files or time periods should be prioritized

- What format would be most useful for the final analysis

If there are more than 20 files, start by analyzing just the 10 most recent files.

Show me the top 3-5 patterns you found with 2-3 specific examples for each pattern. Once I confirm you're on the right track, analyze the remaining files.

weekly_plan.title: Make a weekly plan
weekly_plan.description: Schedule priorities, meetings, and deep work blocks.
weekly_plan.prompt:
Create a practical weekly plan for me.

Inputs:
- Top goals (max 3):
- Fixed meetings/commitments:
- Deep work windows:
- Must-do tasks:
- Nice-to-have tasks:

Output:
1) Priority order
2) Day-by-day plan (2–3 core tasks per day)
3) Risk + buffer time suggestions
4) 5-minute daily wrap-up checklist

meeting_minutes.title: Write meeting minutes
meeting_minutes.description: Summarize decisions, action items, and open questions.
meeting_minutes.prompt:
Turn my meeting notes into clear meeting minutes.

Topic:
Attendees:
Time:
Raw notes (paste here):

Output:
1) Key outcomes (3–6)
2) Decisions
3) Action items (owner / due date / acceptance criteria)
4) Risks + open questions
5) Suggested agenda for the next meeting

reply_email.title: Reply to an email
reply_email.description: Generate a polite, clear email template (two tones).
reply_email.prompt:
Help me write an email reply.

Original email (paste here):
My goal (confirm / decline / push forward / clarify):
Tone (formal / friendly / firm but polite):
Key points to include:

Output:
1) Subject line suggestions
2) Body (2 versions: more formal / more concise)
3) Questions for the recipient to confirm (if any)

make_docx.title: Draft a DOCX
make_docx.description: Write a structured document and export as DOCX.
make_docx.prompt:
Help me write a document and export it as a .docx file.

Document type (PRD / proposal / meeting summary / report / SOP):
Audience:
Tone (formal / friendly / concise):
Must-include points:
Length target:

Deliverable:
1) Suggested outline
2) Full content
3) Export as a .docx file (save it under artifacts/)

make_spreadsheet.title: Design a spreadsheet
make_spreadsheet.description: Define fields and formulas for reusable tracking.
make_spreadsheet.prompt:
Design a spreadsheet schema (columns + example rows) for the following purpose.

Purpose:
Data sources:
Metrics / aggregation needs:
Preferred output (weekly report / dashboard / checklist):

Output:
1) Recommended columns (type + notes)
2) Example data (3–5 rows)
3) Useful formulas / pivot suggestions
4) Data validation + filling guidelines

budget_plan.title: Create a budget plan
budget_plan.description: Allocate by category + timeline with review rules.
budget_plan.prompt:
Help me create a budget plan.

Context:
- Budget period (monthly / quarterly / project):
- Total budget:
- Fixed costs:
- Variable costs:
- Goal (save money / control spending / save for something):

Output:
1) Suggested categories + ratios
2) A category budget table (copy-paste friendly)
3) Overrun rules + adjustment plan
4) Weekly/monthly review checklist
